Title: Woman found stuffed in suitcase died from slash wounds to the head, say police
Article:
KAJANG: The unidentified woman whose body was found inside a suitcase in a home in Taman Impian Indah here on Sunday died from slash wounds to the head.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said this was confirmed following a post-mortem conducted at the Kajang Hospital Forensic Unit at 6.20pm yesterday.
"The post-mortem also found that no parts of the body were detached or severed," he said in an update today.
He said the unit had been rented to a foreign married couple since February.
Investigations are ongoing to determine if the body belongs to the female tenant.
Yesterday, it was reported that police received a report on the
discovery of an unidentified body
at a house in Taman Impian Indah at 8.37pm on Sunday.
This comes after a homeowner made the grisly discovery when he checked on the apartment after failing to contact his tenants.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman jailed, daughter and son-in-law claim trial over beer theft
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: A 50-year-old woman was jailed for four months by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to stealing three cartons of beer with her daughter and son-in-law last month.
Tamil Selvi, 50, entered the plea after the charge was read to her before Magistrate Aini Adilah Muhammad Faizal.
Her daughter, N. Tawamani, 23, and son-in-law, V. Thayaran, 26, pleaded not guilty to the same charge.
The trio were jointly charged with stealing three cartons of beer worth RM431.40 at a 99 Speedmart outlet in Jalan Pantai Bersih at 9.30pm on June 12.
The offence, under Section 380 of the Penal Code for theft in a building, carries a jail term of up to 10 years and a fine upon conviction.
The court sentenced Tamil to four months' jail from the date of her arrest.
The court was told that Tamil, who was represented by a National Legal Aid Foundation lawyer, worked as a maid earning RM800 a month and was supporting seven dependents.
Lawyer Y. Anbananthan, representing Tawamani and Thayaran, applied for a low bail, saying Thayaran earned RM1,500 a month as a factory worker while Tawamani was a housewife.
Anbananthan said the defence would submit a representation to the prosecution, as Tamil had already pleaded guilty.
Aini Adilah granted Tawamani and Thayaran RM3,000 bail each with one surety and fixed July 27 for mention.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Siti Nur Farihin Ahmad prosecuted.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang plans slaughterhouses in every district to boost livestock industry
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang plans to establish a slaughterhouse in every district to strengthen the state's livestock industry and enhance food safety standards.
State Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman Datuk Rashidi Zinol said the initiative is aimed at creating a more organised, hygienic and syariah-compliant slaughtering ecosystem while supporting the state's modern and sustainable agri-food agenda.
He said the proposed district slaughterhouses would be developed through strategic collaboration between the state government, the Penang Veterinary Services Department, the local government division under state Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman Jason H'ng Mooi Lye and the Penang Islamic Religious Affairs Department under Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid.
"The collaboration will ensure the development and operation of the slaughterhouses meet technical requirements, public health standards, waste management needs, animal welfare requirements and syariah compliance," he said in a statement today.
Rashidi said slaughtering activities in several areas were currently carried out on a small scale according to local needs, but the state believed a more systematic and centralised approach was needed to ensure uniform standards statewide.
The proposed facilities will serve as slaughtering centres for cattle, goats and buffaloes, while also functioning as support hubs offering veterinary inspections, animal health monitoring, disease control, efficient waste management and processing facilities that comply with food hygiene and safety standards.
Rashidi said all livestock brought to the centres would undergo veterinary inspection before and after slaughter to ensure only healthy animals entered the food supply chain.
The centres will also strengthen compliance with halal slaughtering procedures through closer monitoring of certified slaughterers, slaughtering methods and syariah requirements by the state's religious authorities.
Rashidi said the initiative is expected to benefit livestock farmers and entrepreneurs by providing easier access to modern facilities, enabling them to improve productivity and expand market opportunities.
"The project will also support Penang's food security agenda by improving the resilience of the state's food production and processing system amid global supply chain disruptions, rising production costs and economic uncertainty.
"Beyond ensuring a safe and quality meat supply, the slaughterhouses are expected to spur downstream livestock-based industries such as food processing, frozen products, packaging and halal logistics, creating employment opportunities and increasing entrepreneurs' income," he added.
He said environmental sustainability would also be incorporated into the project through efficient slaughter waste management systems, pollution control measures and compliance with requirements set by local authorities and relevant agencies.
Rashidi said stakeholder engagement sessions involving livestock farmers, industry players, local authorities and technical agencies would be held to determine suitable locations, capacity and operating models for each district.
He said the state government remained committed to strengthening Penang's agri-food sector through inclusive and high-impact initiatives, describing the proposed slaughterhouses as a long-term investment in the future of the livestock industry, food safety and the wellbeing of the people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two soldiers injured by debris during weapons training at Hobart Camp
Article:
ALOR STAR: Two soldiers were injured after being struck by debris during a weapons training exercise at Hobart Camp here yesterday.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the incident occurred at 6.05pm while the victims, a sergeant and a corporal, were taking part in a multi-weapon training session involving other participants.
He said the training, which began on June 28, is scheduled to end on July 2 and involves several phases covering both light and heavy weapons.
"The 29-year-old trainee, a corporal, sustained left knee injuries. Meanwhile, the 35-year-old instructor, a sergeant with 16 years of military service and nine years of coaching experience, suffered an injury to his right thigh," he said today.
Adzli added that doctors confirmed the injuries were caused by bouncing debris, such as rocks ricocheting during an unexpected explosion, rather than direct gunfire.
He explained that the heavy weapon had stopped firing automatically due to a technical hitch.
The explosion occurred suddenly when the equipment was being inspected.
The instructor has been discharged from Sik Hospital, while the trainee was transferred to Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital (HSAH) for specialised evaluation due to concerns over nerve damage and severe blood loss.
"The case is being investigated under Section 37 of the Arms Act 1960 for causing injury while discharging a firearm. Investigators from the Kedah police forensics unit and the military are currently conducting a joint scene investigation."
Adzli said a separate investigation into the June 16 grenade explosion at the same camp, which claimed the lives of two soldiers, has been formally handed over to the military.
The police have classified the prior case as sudden death.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shell expects 65pct rise in global LNG demand by 2050
Article:
LONDON/SINGAPORE: Global liquefied natural gas demand is expected to rise by around 65 per cent by 2050, driven largely by Asia as countries seek lower-emission alternatives to coal and data centres boost power demand, Shell said in an annual report on Tuesday.
Global demand is likely to reach nearly 700 million metric tons a year by that date, the world's largest trader of the superchilled fuel said in its 2026 LNG Outlook.
LNG trade, which reached 422 million tons in 2025, had been set to increase in 2026, it added. However, severe disruption to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has shut in around one-fifth of global monthly LNG supply since the Middle East conflict began.
"The conflict created a system-wide shock with disruption cascading across all segments of the economy, but the LNG industry has proved resilient and able to adapt to changing market conditions," Cederic Cremers, Shell's president of integrated gas, said in the report.
The company said recent growth in LNG supply and regasification infrastructure had improved market resilience and helped limit the impact of the disruption to shipping through Hormuz.
In addition, the ramp-up of new liquefaction facilities in North America, improved performance at existing plants and slower Asian LNG imports have helped offset reduced supply from the Middle East.
The US-Israeli war on Iran has disrupted the global LNG outlook, driving up prices, damaging Qatar's export facilities and delaying new supply, casting doubt on demand from price-sensitive Asian buyers.
Analysts expect higher prices to curb South Asian demand, with buyers turning to alternative LNG sources or switching to coal and domestic gas.
Asian LNG imports for the first half of 2026 are down nearly 4 per cent to 127.70 million tons compared with the corresponding period last year, according to data from analytics firm Kpler.
Although Asian LNG spot prices rose above US$20 per million British thermal units at the peak of the Middle East crisis, they remained well below levels seen in 2022 following Russia's invasion of Ukraine, reflecting greater resilience in the LNG market, Shell said.
Asian spot LNG prices were last at US$15.35/mmBtu, a near four-month low as the market stayed hopeful about a peace deal to end the conflict.
NEED FOR NEW INVESTMENTS
About 180 million tons per year of new LNG supply is forecast to enter the market by 2030, improving the availability and affordability of gas and opening up demand in new markets.
Forecasts show South and Southeast Asia will account for around 40 per cent of global LNG imports by 2050 as countries seek lower-emission alternatives to coal to meet rapidly growing energy demand.
In more mature Asian markets such as Japan, data centres are emerging as a new source of power demand, the report said.
LNG will also continue to play a key role in European energy security and help balance intermittent renewable power generation as domestic gas production declines, Shell said.
To meet rising demand, significant additional investment will be needed in new LNG export projects through the 2030s and 2040s, with around 200 million tons per year of new supply required in addition to projects already under construction.
"While more investment in both supply and demand infrastructure is needed, the long-term outlook remains strong and LNG will continue to be a stabilising force in the global energy system," Cremers said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas strikes new oil discoveries in Suriname
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), through its unit Petronas Suriname E&P BV, has made two new discoveries and completed a successful appraisal at offshore Block 52 in Suriname.
The latest achievements raise the company's total to eight successful wells in the country, collectively unlocking more than one billion barrels of oil equivalent in recoverable resources, Petronas said in a statement.
The Caiman-1 exploration well, drilled in 90 metres of water to a depth of 5,065 metres, encountered multiple oil-bearing Cretaceous sandstone intervals.
The Swartzia Aspasia Complex-1 (SAC-1) exploration well, located eight kilometres east of Sloanea-1 gas discovery in 610 metres of water depth, and drilled to a total depth of 4,560 metres, intersected gas-bearing sandstone reservoirs.
Petronas said the drill stem testing (DST) demonstrated strong gas deliverability, which indicates good reservoir quality.
"The Roystonea-2 appraisal well, drilled seven kilometres north of Roystonea-1, confirmed the lateral extent of oil-bearing reservoirs, with DST results indicating strong oil productivity, further validating the quality and extent of the reservoir system."
Petronas chief operating officer and executive vice president and chief executive officer of upstream Mohd Jukris Abdul Wahab said the eight successful wells in Suriname demonstrates the company's technical expertise, disciplined execution and strong partnerships.
"Block 52 sits within a highly prospective corridor, the Golden Lane, and we remain focused on translating this resource base into long-term value for Suriname and for Petronas.
"Petronas remains committed to Suriname's broader ambitions in building local capability, developing human capital and creating shared value for both our nations," he said.
The national oil and gas company currently holds interests in eight offshore blocks in Suriname - Blocks 9, 10, 48, 52, 53, 63, 64, and 66.
It operates Block 52 with 80 per cent participating interest, while the remaining 20 per cent is held by Paradise Oil Company NV, a wholly-owned unit of Staatsolie Maatschappij Suriname NV.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zambry: LRT3 to ease commute, cut costs for university students
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The newly opened Shah Alam LRT3 line will make commuting easier and more affordable for university students while helping to ease traffic congestion.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir, who travelled on the LRT3 from Shah Alam Stadium to the Shah Alam campus of Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) ahead of the Semiconductor @ UiTM Initiative launch, said the rail service would provide significant benefits to students commuting within the Klang Valley.
"The LRT provides excellent convenience and will reduce congestion for students, while lowering their travel costs when moving around the Klang Valley," he told reporters.
He said he observed many students using the service and welcomed their positive response.
"I saw many students who were very excited earlier. It certainly provides relief to those who have to deal with traffic congestion when travelling to the Shah Alam campus," he said.
Zambry said improved public transport connectivity would not only enhance students' daily commute but also make access to higher education institutions more efficient.
Following its launch yesterday, the Shah Alam LRT3 line has seen over
10,000 commuters
and is expected to benefit more than two million users.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang fitness trainer sets third world record with 18-hour cardio and weightlifting feat
Article:
LIPIS: Fitness instructor K. Jey Prabakaran Thevar has once again pushed the boundaries of human endurance by setting a new world record for the longest non-stop cardio and weightlifting workout session.
The 29-year-old achieved his third major record by completing an 18-hour session at a gym here on June 28, following previous Malaysian and Asian achievements.
The Kuala Lipis-born athlete first entered the record books in 2023 with a 12-hour non-stop cardio workout before extending the mark to 15 hours, earning recognition from the Guinness World Records.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2024/10/1123544/pahang-fitness-trainer-eyes-guinness-world-record
Jey Prabakaran said his latest attempt began about 4am and ended around 11pm on June 28, officially clocking 18 hours and securing entries in the Malaysia Book of Records and the Influencer Book of World Records.
The youngest of four siblings said preparing for the latest feat was not easy as it required extraordinary physical endurance, mental strength and determination.
"It was challenging to remain focused as I began the session early in the morning, with judges closely monitoring me to ensure I met all the requirements
"As the hours passed, I became more comfortable, and it gradually turned into a routine when some gym-going friends joined me later in the evening. Surrounded by supportive friends, the fatigue seemed to disappear and kept me going," he said today.
Jey Prabakaran, fondly known as 'Dr Kjey', dedicated his third world record to his late father, Kunasigaran Suppiya Thevar, who passed away in 1999 when Jey Prabakaran was three years old.
"This was not just a record-breaking attempt but a tribute from a son to a father who left too soon. Even though my father was not physically present, I believe he has always been by my side throughout my journey," he said.
Jey Prabakaran expressed gratitude to his family, friends and supporters who stood by him throughout his journey, especially during difficult moments when some doubted his ability to achieve the feat.
He hopes his record-breaking achievements will inspire Malaysians, particularly the younger generation, to believe that no dream is impossible with discipline, perseverance and sacrifice.
Jey Prabakaran
, who completed 3,000 jumping jacks in three hours during last year's National Sports Day celebration, now plans to attempt 5,000 jumping jacks in five hours during the state-level celebration in October.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Contract law reform to boost investor confidence, slash commercial disputes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The proposed reform of contract law will increase legal certainty, reduce transaction costs and commercial disputes, said Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said.
She told Parliament today that it would strengthen investor confidence, and align the country's commercial framework with Asean and global standards in the digital economy era.
Azalina, the Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform), delivered a briefing on the final report of the Study on the Reform of Contract Law in Malaysia.
She described the reform effort as a strategic step by the Madani Government to refine the country's commercial legal ecosystem.
She said the proposed bills are planned to be tabled in Parliament at the next sitting, subject to the cabinet's decision.
The final report stems from a comprehensive review of the Contracts Act, Malaysia's principal law governing the formation, performance and termination of contracts.
Azalina said the government had taken proactive steps to address the Act's shortcomings by establishing the Committee on the Review and Study of Contract Law Reform on Jan 14, 2025, to lead the study and put forward reform recommendations.
She said eight working groups were established to examine all 191 provisions of the Act, each led by one or two committee members and supported by eight to ten experts from various sectors.
The final report has been tabled for the reference of members of parliament.
Azalina said the report consolidated three main studies into a single comprehensive document: a review and reform of the Act itself, proposing modernised language, structure and existing legal doctrines; a proposed Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Bill to recognise the rights of third parties to enforce contractual terms drafted for their benefit, a right not currently provided for explicitly under the Act; and a proposed Agency Bill to replace Part X of the Act with a more modern agency framework, including consideration of emerging issues such as the use of artificial intelligence in commercial transactions.
Azalina said the Legal Affairs Division of the Prime Minister's Department will organise a special briefing session for all MPs, to be delivered directly by committee members, with the date to be announced later.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taiwan raids tech firms in China AI chip smuggling probe
Article:
TAIPEI: Taiwanese investigators have raided the Taiwan offices of US company Super Micro Computer and two other tech firms, a prosecutor said Tuesday, as part of an expanded probe into the alleged smuggling of Nvidia AI chips to China.
Prosecutors said in May they were investigating the shipment of "high-end" AI servers containing advanced Nvidia chips to China, Macau and Hong Kong, in violation of US export controls.
Nine people are now under investigation, up from three previously, Huang Sheng, head prosecutor in the Keelung Prosecutors Office, told AFP.
They are accused of forging documents so they could ship roughly 50 servers made by Super Micro Computer to China.
Some of the servers were cleared by Taiwan customs and sent to China via Japan, an official previously told AFP on the condition of anonymity.
Twelve sites were raided on Monday as part of the probe, the prosecutors office said in a statement.
They included the homes of six people and offices of the companies they worked for – Nasdaq-listed Super Micro Computer and Taiwan-listed firms Albatron Technology and Chief Telecom.
The United States restricts the export of its most cutting-edge AI chips to China, partly over concerns the technology could be used by Beijing's military.
But it is not a criminal offence in Taiwan – a situation lawmakers and experts say needs to change – with Taiwanese prosecutors relying on other laws to go after offenders.
Lawmaker Chung Chia-pin, who belongs to President Lai Ching-te's Democratic Progressive Party (DPP), plans to propose an amendment to the Foreign Trade Act to include a "mainland China semiconductor chip clause" that would make exporting chips there illegal.
Chung told AFP Tuesday that a loophole in the law was created under former president Ma Ying-jeou, who belongs to the Kuomintang party, and successive DPP-led governments have failed to close it.
Top-end chips made by US titan Nvidia – the world's most valuable company – are used to train and run AI systems.
In response to Washington's export restrictions, China has been accelerating efforts to develop its own AI chips and break away from reliance on US hardware.
This month, Taiwanese Deputy Economic Affairs Minister Ho Chin-tsang said Taiwan and the United States "will work to implement our shared export control goals", but the government has not provided details.
Chris McGuire, an expert on China and AI at the US-based Council on Foreign Relations, said chip smuggling was a "really significant problem" in Taiwan and Southeast Asia.
"It's really, really important that allies align with the United States on all of these policies and also legal authorities," McGuire, who worked at the National Security Council under former US president Joe Biden, told a forum in Taipei this month.
"It's not a criminal violation in Taiwan to export AI chips to China, obviously it is under US law, but it's not under Taiwanese law. That needs to change, right?"
Super Micro Computer, Albatron Technology and Chief Telecom have said separately they are cooperating with investigators. Their shares have seen sharp falls this week.
Prosecutors say it is too early to know if the case is linked to a Nvidia chip smuggling case involving Super Micro Computer employees in the United States.
A US indictment unsealed in March showed employees of the company allegedly raked in billions of dollars diverting Nvidia AI chips to China in breach of export controls.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Amir: Health Ministry budget rationalisation will not affect healthcare reforms
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The rationalisation of the Health Ministry's operating expenditure will not affect the implementation of the Medical and Health Insurance/Takaful (MHIT) Plan or the RESET strategy.
Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the proposed adjustment involved only non-critical expenditure and would not affect the ministry's core spending approved under the 2026 Budget.
He said the Health Ministry had revised the proposed adjustment to RM500 million in operating expenditure, down from the original proposal of RM3.1 billion.
The move, he said, was aimed at creating fiscal space to absorb part of the increase in subsidy spending following the crisis in the Middle East.
"This amount does not involve key expenditures such as emoluments, medicine supplies, overtime allowances, including on-call allowances, and other essential spending.
"The allocation for medicines has also been increased to RM6.5 billion this year, compared with RM6 billion in 2025.
"This year, the Health Ministry is also targeting the recruitment of more than 18,000 healthcare personnel, with no reduction compared with the previous year.
"This proves that the cost-saving measures will not affect the government's priority of ensuring that the people continue to receive quality healthcare," he said during Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.
He was responding to a supplementary question from Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad (BN-Simpang Renggam) on whether the Joint Committee overseeing the RESET strategy and the MHIT Plan would be affected following the reduction in the Health Ministry's allocation.
RESET refers to five strategic pillars: improving MHIT offerings; enhancing price transparency; strengthening the digital health system; expanding access to cost-effective treatment options; and reforming healthcare provider payment mechanisms.
Amir Hamzah said the current fiscal situation made the RESET agenda even more important and relevant, as it aims to ensure healthcare costs remain sustainable over the long term.
"Initiatives such as diagnosis-related group (DRG)-based payments and electronic medical records (EMR) are not only aimed at addressing medical inflation.
"They also reduce wastage by preventing unnecessary repeat diagnostic tests and ensuring that treatment payments are more transparent and value-based," he said.
He added that the efficiency-driven approach was in line with the government's efforts through the enforcement of the Government Procurement Act 2026, which is expected to come into force in 2027, to curb leakages, inefficiencies and wastage of public funds.
He said the improved efficiency would allow more public funds to be channelled towards service delivery for the people.
"In the context of private healthcare, the same principle is applied through strategic purchasing and the DRG system under the Basic MHIT Plan to ensure fair and efficient pricing for hospital treatment.
"These cost-saving measures will not undermine the Joint Committee's ability to implement the RESET strategy and the MHIT Plan.
"Instead, they further strengthen the government's commitment to providing a more affordable, efficient and sustainable healthcare system for the rakyat," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Russia pounds on the gates of Ukraine's 'fortress belt'
Article:
RUSSIA is grinding its way into Kostiantynivka, a key stronghold in Ukraine's eastern "fortress belt" long coveted by the Kremlin, even as its gains across the rest of the 1,200-km front line have largely stalled.
Fighting has begun to seep into the city itself. Small groups of Russian soldiers are attempting to infiltrate its outskirts, senior Ukrainian commanders said last week, suggesting close-quarters assaults could follow.
Kostiantynivka is the southernmost of four key settlements forming a defensive line central to Ukraine's effort to hold the heavily industrialised Donetsk region.
The push towards it underscores Moscow's enduring manpower advantage, even as Ukrainian mid-range drone strikes on logistics have weakened its fighting capabilities, analysts said.
"The effect (of mid-range strikes) hasn't been so great that it would have forced the Russians to suspend their offensive," said Emil Kastehelmi of the Black Bird conflict analysis team in Finland.
"So even though Russia has been taking increasingly heavy losses in the rear, they are still able to continue their offensives, at least in certain sectors."
Occupying Kostiantynivka would provide Russian forces a foothold from which to move north along the belt, now the central axis of their campaign.
But any advance would likely be long and bloody for its forces, in a possible echo of other costly sieges of eastern cities like Pokrovsk and Avdiivka.
President Vladimir Putin has insisted Russia must control all of Donetsk before the war ends. Ukraine still holds around a fifth of the region after more than four years of fighting.
Putin said last week Russia was close to capturing Kostiantynivka, whose pre-war population of nearly 70,000 has fallen to around 2,000.
In comments to Ukrainian media, senior commanders of Kyiv's 19th Army Corps dismissed that claim as an exaggeration and said their troops were picking off small groups of Russians that had managed to enter.
Maj. Gen. Viktor Nikoliuk, the head of Ukraine's eastern operational command, told Ukraine's public broadcaster on Thursday that Kostiantynivka could hold out at the current rate of manpower and resources.
[[nid:1476810]]
While the tactical situation is worsening for Ukraine, the Russian infiltrations are not enough for "a rapid operational breakthrough", the US-based Institute for the Study of War said in a June 23 assessment.
Still, Russian efforts to envelop the city through pincer movements are steadily raising the cost for Kyiv of defending it, said Ukrainian analyst Ruslan Mykula, of the DeepState open-source mapping group.
"A choice will have to be made: either raise the stakes or withdraw," he said. "And right now, the situation is such that the stakes are rising with each passing day."
Kastehelmi said the city's fall "seems to be more of a question of time."
Moscow's troops are also encroaching on the northern end of the fortress belt, threatening the cities of Sloviansk and Kramatorsk with frequent air and drone attacks from around 15 km away.
Ukrainian supply routes are already under sustained pressure, with artillery, drones and guided bombs battering infrastructure along the road north from Kostiantynivka, troops in the area said.
Reuters recently joined members of the "Predator" rifle brigade under the National Police, assigned to patrol the embattled route against drones and remotely dropped mines.
Strands of fibre-optic cable, used to guide first-person-view drones, are strewn across anti-drone netting draped over the road, glistening under the scorching sun.
Ground robots carrying food, water and supplies - now the predominant method of delivery inside the so-called "kill zone" - trundle back and forth, as soldiers speed past on quad bikes.
The route is too dangerous to evacuate the dead and wounded on standard vehicles, said 34-year-old serviceman Oleksandr Kosmin: "Everything happens on foot."
Civilian life nearby is collapsing under the pressure. In Druzhkivka, around 12 km north, residents are being forced to leave as the fighting edges closer.
[[nid:1476809]]
On one leafy street, a husband and wife were slumped, dead, inside a van struck by a Russian drone. White ribbons, meant to mark the vehicle as civilian, still fluttered on its roof.
"Why am I leaving? Because I'm scared. Drones are flying," said Larysa Sereda, 59, speaking from a police evacuation van.
"But I plan to return home. I don't want to stay in some strange place. The war will end, and I'll come home."
The creeping Russian gains around Kostiantynivka come despite mounting strains on its war effort caused by Ukrainian attacks on supply lines to and from Crimea, as well as longer-range strikes on the oil sector.
Russian-installed authorities on the occupied Black Sea peninsula have imposed a state of emergency to deal with economic issues, and have halted all fuel sales to individuals and businesses.
On the battlefield more broadly, Russian forces appear overextended and frontline assaults often amount to just one or two soldiers, said Mykula, the Ukrainian analyst.
In comments to Reuters, however, Denis Pushilin, the Kremlin-installed head of Ukraine's eastern Donetsk region, said Russia's campaign to capture more cities was continuing.
"Talking about whether this is happening slowly or quickly isn't really the point," he said.
Russian hardliners have urged Putin to abandon the US-backed peace process and escalate his war as Ukraine's strikes intensify, including in Moscow.
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Semiconductor@UiTM launched to boost tech talent through industry-integrated learning
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Higher Education Ministry has expressed confidence that Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) is capable of implementing a major agenda in expanding the semiconductor field through the launch of the Semiconductor@UiTM Initiative.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the semiconductor field is a high-impact programme and difficult, yet UiTM has shown serious commitment to its implementation.
"The prime minister (Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim) previously announced that the government allocated RM20 million in the recent budget to ensure this initiative can be executed.
"This underscores the critical importance of the industry before us. The government has also implemented and introduced the National Semiconductor Strategy (NSS) as a national action plan to ensure our country remains ahead with a good ecosystem.
"But to ensure this situation is sustainable, we must have a good ecosystem. An ecosystem that can be integrated includes cutting-edge education.
"This is what we are giving to UiTM as an example. While we previously gave this responsibility to Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM), we are confident that UiTM is capable of hosting and implementing this major agenda alongside USM and other universities.
"Therefore, UiTM needs to shift in terms of its curriculum, which I earlier referred to as its cutting-edge education, because you need to be ahead. If our curriculum is still anchored to the old system, we will not be able to compete with what lies ahead," he said.
Zambry said this in his speech while officiating the Launching Ceremony of the Semiconductor@UiTM Initiative at UiTM Shah Alam here today.
Zambry noted that the industry is highly crucial to Malaysia given that the nation is among the world's largest exporters of semiconductors.
Malaysia, he said, accounts for 13 per cent of global semiconductor assembly, testing and packaging (ATP) activities, while the electrical and electronics (E&E) sector contributes more than 40 per cent of the country's total exports.
He added that in 2025 alone, semiconductor exports were projected to reach almost RM390 billion in value, highlighting the industry's role as a key driver of economic growth.
Zambry also urged universities to expand Work-Based Learning (WBL), saying students should gain practical experience in industry rather than rely solely on classroom-based teaching.
"As I also mentioned in my previous speech, the latest policy is to expand our educational concept beyond just being on campus, allowing them to also be placed in identified key industries.
"Currently, many universities, particularly involving our technical universities — we have five technical universities, now including UniKL — are all using this approach.
"This means they also learn within the industries, not just by going out to the industry, but industries are also coming to the campuses. So, industry and campus exist together in those areas, including at UiTM.
"We are adopting this approach to enable a method where learning to gain knowledge and education is no longer confined to the campus, especially in technical fields," he said.
The Semiconductor@UiTM Initiative includes specialised laboratories for integrated circuit (IC) design, semiconductor fabrication, device testing and measurement, and process automation.
In an accompanying statement, UiTM said it has more than 3,000 E&E students and aims to double its intake with the upgraded facilities.
It added that the university has also forged partnerships with various stakeholders including Intel, AMD, Collaborative Research in Engineering, Science and Technology (CREST), the Advanced Semiconductor Academy of Malaysia (ASEM), the Penang Skills Development Centre (PSDC) and several local technology firms to provide students with greater industry exposure and hands-on training.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysians injured in Thai bomb blast were not intended targets
Article:
KOTA BARU: The two Malaysians injured in a bomb blast in Tak Bai, Narathiwat, were not the intended targets of the attack, which Thai police believe was carried out by a separatist group.
Narathiwat provincial police commander Major General Prayong Kotsakha said investigators believed the improvised explosive device had been planted for another vehicle.
"There were several vehicles on the road at the time. The two Malaysians were not the actual target.
"Their vehicle overtook the intended target before the explosion occurred," he said when contacted.
Prayong said preliminary investigations indicated the attack was carried out by members of the Barisan Revolusi Nasional (BRN), a separatist group active in southern Thailand.
He said investigations were ongoing.
Yesterday, Abdullah Syarapi Abd Rahman, 45, and Muhammad Yusri Udin, 38, were injured when a
roadside bomb exploded
beneath a culvert in Tak Bai.
Abdullah suffered shrapnel wounds to his forehead, a serious injury to his left leg and head injuries, while Yusri sustained minor injuries.
Both men were initially treated at Narathiwat Hospital before being transferred to Hospital Raja Perempuan Zainab II in Kota Baru today for further treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara: Malaysia's official reserve assets at US$130.63bil at end-May 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's official reserve assets amounted to US$130.63 billion at the end of May 2026, compared with US$129.73 billion at the end of April 2026.
Bank Negara Malaysia said other foreign currency assets amounted to US$766.5 million.
The central bank said the pre-determined short-term outflows of foreign currency loans, securities and deposits for the next 12 months, which include, among others, the scheduled repayment of external borrowings by the government and the maturity of foreign currency Bank Negara Interbank Bills, amounted to US$6.65 billion.
"The net short forward positions amounted to US$24.67 billion as at the end of May 2026, reflecting the management of ringgit liquidity in the money market," Bank Negara said in a statement today.
Bank Negara said, in line with the practice adopted since April 2006, the data excludes projected foreign currency inflows arising from interest income and the drawdown of project loans.
These projected foreign currency inflows amount to US$3.04 billion over the next 12 months.
Meanwhile, the only contingent short-term net drain on foreign currency assets is government guarantees of foreign currency debt due within one year, which amount to US$846.4 million.
"There are no foreign currency loans with embedded options, and no undrawn, unconditional credit lines provided by or to other central banks, international organisations, banks and other financial institutions.
"Bank Negara also does not engage in foreign currency options vis-à-vis the ringgit," the central bank said.
Overall, Bank Negara said the detailed breakdown of international reserves under the International Monetary Fund Special Data Dissemination Standard format indicates that, as at the end of May 2026, Malaysia's international reserves remain usable.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three NGOs urge government to halt Cybercrimes Bill, seek PSSC review
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Three non-governmental organisations (NGOs) have urged the government to halt the second reading of the Cybercrimes Bill 2026 and refer it to a parliamentary special select committee (PSSC).
NGOs Article 19, the Centre for Independent Journalism (CIJ) and Sinar Project expressed concerns that the cybercrime legislation failed to meet minimum standards set out in the United Nations Cybercrime Convention.
"We call on the government to halt the second reading of the bill and send the bill to the PSSC on Human Rights, Elections, and Institutional Reform for further review and consultation to address the shortcomings identified above to ensure the compatibility of any cybercrime legislation with international standards of freedom of expression," it said in a joint statement today.
The bill covers offences including identity theft, computer-related fraud and forgery, transmission of manipulated content, and dissemination of intimate images — replacing the Computer Crimes Act 1997.
The NGOs said the bill lacked prior judicial authorisation for surveillance, data access, searches and seizures. They added that there were no clear limits on data retention, and it did not provide explicit protection for privileged communications, such as journalists' sources.
The three bodies said the bill must include clear human rights protections and safeguards that balance security and fundamental rights.
Section 36 of the bill provides vast powers to authorities, allowing authorised officers conducting a search to demand passwords, encryption or decryption codes, and software or hardware to access information.
"Access without thorough, independent judicial oversight violates privacy rights.
"We note that under international standards, encryption facilitates the exercise of free expression and privacy, and restrictions on encryption and anonymity must meet the three-part test for limitations on the right to freedom of expression under international law," it said.
Further, sections 38 and 39 allow authorised officers to order the preservation and forced disclosure of user data without any judicial oversight while prohibiting persons issued with such written notice from communicating its existence and content.
"This means that those who receive such a preservation notice cannot disclose anything about it or even seek judicial oversight or remedy.
"It is unclear whether communicating the notice to one's legal counsel would be a violation – on its face, it would be," the statement read.
Failing to comply with either provision may result in a fine of RM1 million.
"This sanction is disproportionately high, increases the coercive effect of the order and reinforces the chilling effect of the gag provisions," they added.
The groups said these provisions not only could prevent or impede the right to a fair trial, but also violate Articles 23 and 24 of the UN Cybercrime Convention, which require states to ensure that their rules and procedures protect human rights.
"This includes the principle of proportionality and the provision of conditions such as independent reviews, the right to an effective remedy, clear justifications for the use of these powers, and limits on their duration," the NGOs said.
Additionally, any police officer, public officer, or officer of the Malaysian Communication and Multimedia Commission can become an 'authorised' officer at the minister's determination, under section 26.
Under section 27(2), any person who is not a police officer may be granted 'the powers of a police officer of whatever rank as provided for under the Criminal Procedure Code' for investigating any offence.
The three NGOs also said Section 23, which addresses deepfakes among others, uses broadly framed language to tackle cybercrime, which may also capture legitimate forms of free speech — including satire, art, journalism or comments critical of the government.
"This concern is especially significant in Malaysia, where those who create satire and dissent continue to be targeted and legally harassed," the statement said.
Yesterday, the
Malaysian Media Council
also urged the bill to be referred to a parliamentary select committee for further scrutiny and improvement, as it infringes on privacy, including using covert operations to obtain private communications.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Umno rubbishes claim BN will win fewer than 40 seats
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Allegations that Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) is faltering are merely political narratives detached from reality, said Johor Umno information chief Md Israk Abdullah.
Responding to claims by DAP strategic director Liew Chin Tong that BN is weak and only capable of winning fewer than 40 seats, Israk said the remarks do not reflect the situation on the ground.
"While we are on the ground meeting the people, listening to their heartbeat, and bringing offers for the future of Johor, DAP continues to be busy campaigning by creating perceptions and playing with political sentiments," he said in a statement today.
"DAP's accusation that BN is weakening is just a political narrative; it is not based on facts. DAP has failed to reflect the true reality at the grassroots level," he said.
Israk said the momentum among Johoreans is clearly with BN, pointing to a growing 'Gelombang Biru' (blue wave) felt across the state.
He said voters want the continuity of a stable, experienced government with a proven track record.
"Throughout the campaign period, the reception received from the people has been increasingly reassuring that they want to maintain the existing leadership to continue the Johor development agenda. The people judge based on performance, not propaganda," he said.
He said DAP remains free to create perceptions on social media or issue political predictions, but reminded them that Johor voters are mature enough to differentiate between those who only know how to build narratives and those who truly work for the state.
On polling day, the people of Johor themselves will send the clearest signal to Pakatan Harapan and DAP.
"That signal is the Gelombang Biru, which will ensure Johor continues to be led by a stable, competent government that is trusted by the people, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over 58,000 buildings likely damaged or destroyed in Venezuela
Article:
CARACAS: The powerful twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last week damaged or destroyed more than 58,000 buildings, according to a preliminary assessment of satellite data published by US space agency NASA.
Some 1,700 people were killed and thousands remain missing following the quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 – the strongest to hit the South American nation in more than a century.
"Approximately 58,870 buildings were likely damaged or destroyed across the affected region" based on satellite radar data gathered on June 25, the day after the earthquakes, according to researchers Corey Scher and Jamon Van Den Hoek of Oregon State University.
The duo were citing data from the European Space Agency's high-resolution radar imagery satellite Sentinel-1.
"This is a preliminary, rapid assessment. It reflects abrupt surface change consistent with damage," the researchers wrote, adding that the figure should only be read as an indicator and was not verified on the ground.
National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez reported on Monday that 855 buildings have been damaged, including 189 "total collapses."
NASA said that its satellites were "providing critical support, capturing imagery and data to help teams on the ground assess impacts and guide response efforts."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MPs trade blows in Dewan Rakyat over Kelantan child sexual crime statistics
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A heated exchange broke out in the Dewan Rakyat after a member of parliament was accused of citing outdated statistics to claim child sexual crime cases were on the rise in Kelantan.
The shouting match began when RSN Rayer (PH-Jelutong) defended remarks he made yesterday, insisting that reports he had cited — one of which was from the New Straits Times — showed an increase in sexual crime cases in Kelantan.
"As I said yesterday, this is an accusation made against me. When I debated yesterday, I was referring to published reports — one by the New Straits Times, one by Free Malaysia Today — which showed statistics of sexual offence cases in Kelantan that are increasing.
"This is something that cannot be twisted," he said in the Dewan Rakyat here today.
This was as Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform), M. Kulasegaran, was winding up the debate on the Sexual Offences against Children Act.
Rayer said his question to the deputy minister, who is also Ipoh Barat MP, was why the government was not focusing on reducing sexual offences against children, which he said were increasingly rampant in Kelantan.
"What is the government's action? That is my question, not a lie," he said.
This prompted Kulasegaran to repeatedly call for the issue not to be politicised, while defending the reports raised by Rayer.
"That (the reports) cannot be denied. And figures were given," he said.
Datuk Dr Mohd Radzi Md Jidin (PN-Putrajaya) intervened, highlighting that the New Straits Times report cited, titled
"Kelantan among states with highest sexual crime cases, says Bukit Aman CID director"
, was dated March 16, 2023.
He pointed out a more recent report dated May 15, 2024, titled
"Selangor tops in sex crimes for past three years."
"Why? Because its population is high. So, don't politicise this. On child sexual crimes, put aside which state it's happening. We focus on how to solve it. Jelutong, close the book on this. Thank you," he said.
Fadhli Shaari (PN-Pasir Mas) also rebutted the claim, citing child crime statistics from the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) to argue that Kelantan had never topped the list for child sexual crimes.
"That is why I have brought child statistics here from DOSM itself, deputy minister. I want to help the deputy minister answer the wild accusation from Jelutong.
"Kelantan has never appeared in these statistics as the state with the highest rate of any sexual crime involving children," he said.
He accused Rayer of citing media reports without proper statistical backing to score political points ahead of the Johor election.
"Quoting media here and there without any statistics is an academic crime that should not be committed by a member of Parliament, and this is cheap politicking by Jelutong to campaign for the Johor election," he said.
As Deputy Dewan Rakyat Speaker Alice Lau attempted to prevent Datuk Awang Hashim (PN-Pendang) from interjecting and directed Kulasegaran to continue, Awang hurled an insult at Rayer.
"The problem is that idiot (mangkuk hayun) politicised it," he said.
Kulasegaran appealed for calm, saying he was obligated to answer as many legislative questions as possible.
"I am tasked to answer as many questions as asked. If I don't answer these questions, we all lose out.
"Right now, we are just debating politics. Putrajaya yesterday specifically — and I appreciate this — informed us of reports he read, which we investigate. That is correct. But let us move on. Outside — you may speak to me outside," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thai opposition leader goes on trial over royal insult law
Article:
BANGKOK: Thailand's main opposition leader and nine other MPs were among dozens on trial Tuesday, accused of ethics breaches over attempts to reform the kingdom's strict royal insult law.
The 44 defendants, whose trial opened at the Supreme Court, include Natthaphong Ruengpanyawut, whose progressive People's Party came second in February's general election.
An earlier iteration of the party had in 2021 proposed draft legislation to amend Thailand's lese-majeste law, which shields the king and his family from criticism and carries a maximum sentence of up to 15 years per offence.
The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) filed a civil complaint in April to the Supreme Court against 44 current and former politicians, alleging a breach of ethics.
If found guilty, they could face lifetime bans on holding political office and be stripped of their right to vote for 10 years.
On Tuesday, the prosecutor submitted the names of 17 witnesses to the Supreme Court, including officials from parliament and the NACC, with the first expected to take the stand on Aug 25.
None of the MPs on trial were in court, according to an AFP reporter.
The next two hearings were set in September and October.
The accused were previously members of the Move Forward party, which had proposed to amend the lese-majeste law.
Thailand's constitutional court dissolved Move Forward in 2024, ruling that its royal reform pledge amounted to an attempt to overthrow the constitutional monarchy.
Most of its members formed a new party, the People's Party, under which the 10 MPs currently on trial were re-elected in February.
Natthaphong told a news conference in April after the Supreme Court accepted the case that the reform effort was "never intended to undermine the democratic system under the monarchy."
"Our objective goes beyond political careers – it is about ensuring equality for the people," he said.
"We will contest this case in the Supreme Court to defend the rights of MPs within a democratic parliamentary system and to ensure that Thailand's democracy genuinely serves its people."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Local council assures fair treatment for all parties
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Baru City Council (MBJB) is treating all political parties fairly in processing permit applications throughout the 16th Johor state election campaign, its mayor said.
Its mayor Datuk Mohd Haffiz Ahmad said the council's licensing department had established clear guidelines and application procedures to ensure the campaign proceeds smoothly.
He said this includes permits for the installation of campaign materials, such as flags, banners, posters, buntings and other forms of election advertising, to ensure they comply with the council's guidelines.
"The permits are an administrative requirement that reflects a shared commitment to ensuring campaign materials are installed in an orderly manner.
"They must not obstruct the view of road users, be placed in locations that endanger public safety, or damage trees, streetlights, pedestrian bridges, traffic signs or other public property.
"They are also prohibited along protocol roads to preserve the city's image," he said at the MBJB full council meeting at Menara MBJB here today.
Several roads in Johor Baru have been gazetted as protocol roads by the state government, making them subject to stricter regulations on vehicle movement as well as higher standards of maintenance, cleanliness and landscaping.
Haffiz said every applicant is required to sign an undertaking to comply with MBJB's conditions for the installation of election campaign materials.
"As such, I hope this commitment is translated into action on the ground so that all campaign activities are carried out ethically, in an orderly manner and with due respect for the rights of road users and local residents.
"I also urge all parties to play their part in maintaining harmony throughout the campaign period," he said.
He said MBJB hopes all parties will refrain from extreme provocation, spreading false information, making speeches touching on race, religion and royalty (3R) sensitivities, or engaging in actions that could disrupt public order.
He also urged all candidates, political parties and their supporters to act responsibly by keeping campaign areas clean and properly managing waste generated from campaign materials.
"Once the election period ends, all banners, buntings, flags, posters and other campaign materials must be removed and the areas cleaned within the stipulated timeframe," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over 127,000 calls made to Talian Kasih since 2022
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Talian Kasih 15999 helpline has received more than 127,000 calls between 2022 and May this year.
Deputy Women, Family and Community Development Minister Lim Hui Ying said 9,327 calls, or 7.36 per cent, involved complaints and cases related to domestic violence.
Applications for welfare assistance accounted for the highest number of calls at 59,589 (47.04 per cent), followed by requests for counselling services with 35,947 calls (28.38 per cent).
Child-related issues accounted for 14,965 calls (11.81 per cent), while the remaining 16,136 calls (12.74 per cent) fell under other categories.
"For domestic violence cases, Talian Kasih 15999 has recorded a rising trend in complaints every year, from 323 complaints in 2022 to 393 in 2023, 477 in 2024, and a sharp increase to 750 last year.
"As of May this year, 470 domestic violence complaints have already been received," she told the Dewan Rakyat today.
She was responding to a question from Datuk Muslimin Yahaya (PN-Sungai Besar) on the effectiveness of helplines and support centres for domestic violence victims, including the rate of cases that successfully received follow-up intervention.
On the success rate of interventions, Lim said all domestic violence complaints received between 2022 and 2025 had been successfully resolved by the ministry.
"Of the complaints received up to May this year, 406 have been resolved, while the remaining 64 are still undergoing follow-up action.
"Every complaint received is recorded and immediately referred to the relevant District Welfare Department for investigation and appropriate intervention," she said.
She said follow-up assistance provided to victims includes help in obtaining an Emergency Protection Order (EPO) and an Interim Protection Order (IPO), placement in a safe shelter, welfare assistance, counselling services and psychosocial support.
"In addition to serving as an emergency complaint channel, Talian Kasih 15999 also functions as a platform to disseminate information and raise awareness about the law and the rights of domestic violence victims.
"Between 2022 and May this year, the platform received more than 2,000 calls from members of the public seeking information, complaint procedures and a better understanding of domestic violence issues," she said.
The ministry urged the public to continue making use of the integrated helpline and called on people not to remain silent if they know of or witness any domestic violence cases in their communities.
Talian Kasih 15999 operates 24 hours a day and can be accessed via the main hotline at 15999, WhatsApp at 019-2615999, or through the Talian Kasih mobile application, without the need to visit a welfare office in person.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrix Concepts rolls out RM30mil homeownership campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has unveiled a homeownership campaign offering more than RM30 million in savings, rewards and prizes to celebrate its 30th anniversary.
The Matrix 30A HOME Rush Campaign will run from June 30 to Nov 30, 2026, featuring selected residential developments across Negri Sembilan, Johor and the Klang Valley.
Group managing director Kelvin Lee said the campaign is a gesture of appreciation to homeowners and communities who have supported Matrix Concepts' growth over the past three decades.
"Matrix Concepts has grown alongside our homeowners and communities. We have seen families flourish, milestones achieved and dreams realised within the townships we have built.
"This campaign is our way of expressing our heartfelt gratitude to everyone who has placed their trust in us," he said.
As part of the campaign, eligible purchasers of participating projects will have the opportunity to win one of three homes with a combined value of RM1.6 million.
Selected developments will also offer cash rewards of up to RM100,000, alongside a range of incentives including exclusive Matrix Mystery Home Boxes, Signature Kitchen rewards, referral bonuses and interactive activities designed for both homeowners and the public.
The campaign also extends to the wider public through an online game, where participants can compete for prizes such as a 30-gram gold bar.
The celebration will culminate in a grand finale in January 2027, featuring prize draws for the three homes, travel packages, smartphones and home appliances.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Palm oil's future hinges on innovation: Noraini Ahmad
Article:
BANGI: Malaysia's oil palm industry can no longer rely solely on commodity production and must increasingly be driven by technology, innovation and the commercialisation of high-value products.
Plantation and Commodities Minister Datuk Seri Dr Noraini Ahmad said the industry's long-term growth lies in its ability to develop technologies, produce higher value products and accelerate the commercialisation of homegrown innovations capable of competing globally.
"The future of the oil palm industry no longer relies solely on commodity production," she said at the Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) Transfer of Technology (TOT) Programme 2026 here today.
Among the technologies introduced was SawitSCAN, an artificial intelligence (AI) and imaging based system that automatically identifies the ripeness of fresh fruit bunches quickly and accurately.
Noraini said the technology could reduce reliance on subjective manual inspections, speed up fruit grading, improve operational efficiency at palm oil mills and enhance the productivity and competitiveness of Malaysia's oil palm industry.
The event also marked another milestone for the industry with MPOB launching nine new technologies aimed at accelerating commercialisation and creating greater value across the palm oil supply chain.
The board said the new technologies reflect the industry's shift beyond traditional edible oil production, with palm oil increasingly being developed as a feedstock for future food products, nutraceuticals, high value oleochemicals and data centre cooling technologies.
The innovations are expected to strengthen Malaysia's position as a global leader in palm oil innovation while supporting the industry's transition towards higher value, technology driven applications.
Meanwhile, the launch of MPOB F7, a plant polymer-coated controlled-release fertiliser, marks a step towards more sustainable fertiliser management by reducing nutrient loss, improving fertiliser use efficiency and lowering environmental impact.
MPOB said it remains focused on addressing the evolving needs of the palm oil industry in line with the sector's sustainability agenda.
MPOB F7 has been commercialised by Smart Fert Sdn Bhd.
In the food and health sector, MPOB introduced three new technologies: a palm-based meat analogue, palm dietary fibre and a technology to enhance the absorption of vitamin E tocotrienols into the human body.
The technologies are aimed at meeting growing demand for functional foods, alternative nutrition and health products.
MPOB also launched Sawit EcoTherm, a palm-based non-conductive coolant designed as an alternative for cooling systems in high-performance infrastructure such as data centres.
It said the technology could improve energy efficiency and reduce the high water consumption associated with conventional data centre cooling systems.
TOT MPOB 2026 also showcased several strategic innovations developed through MPOB's research and development (R&D).
This includes a palm-based rubber processing oil as an alternative for the rubber industry, a high-viscosity lubricant base oil for industrial applications and a large-scale bio-based mono propylene glycol (MPG) production technology using renewable feedstock.
For plant protection, MPOB said it is developing standard planting materials for its Ganoderma disease resistance screening programme to accelerate breeding efforts for more disease-resistant oil palm varieties, improve productivity and reduce losses caused by basal stem rot disease.
MPOB chairman Datuk Mohamad Helmy Othman Basha said the success of a technology should be measured not by the number of patents registered but by its adoption by industry and its contribution to the economy.
"MPOB does not merely produce innovations in the laboratory.
"Our responsibility is to ensure that every technology is successfully transferred to the industry, commercialised and ultimately benefits the people, entrepreneurs, investors and strengthens the competitiveness of the Malaysian oil palm industry on the world stage," he said.
He said every technology introduced under the TOT MPOB programme has undergone research, scientific validation and economic assessment to ensure it meets industry needs and has commercial potential.
To date, MPOB has developed 364 technologies, of which 241 have been commercialised.
He added that MPOB is strengthening its commercialisation ecosystem through a new Intellectual Property Policy recently approved by the Plantation and Commodities Ministry to protect locally developed innovations and strengthen industry confidence in investing in palm oil technologies.
MPOB said it will continue strengthening its role in palm oil research and innovation, with the aim of supporting developments in green technology, the digital economy and other high-value industries.
The TOT MPOB 2026 programme brought together more than 500 participants, including industry players, entrepreneurs, researchers, investors, and micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs).
The programme also featured business matching sessions, technology demonstrations, palm-based product tasting sessions and briefings on commercialisation funding opportunities by the Science, Technology and Innovation Ministry and the Malaysian Technology Development Corp.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Singapore raises electricity tariffs by 17pct due to Middle East crisis
Article:
SINGAPORE: Singapore state utility SP Group said on Tuesday that it will raise electricity tariffs by 17 per cent in the third quarter of 2026 due to soaring prices of imported natural gas, which accounts for 95 per cent of the city-state's power production.
Here are the details:
* Electricity tariffs before taxes for households will increase by 17 per cent compared with the previous quarter, amounting to S$0.0464 ($0.036) per kilowatt-hour, due to higher energy costs.
* The tariffs are reviewed quarterly based on guidelines set by the nation's Energy Markets Authority.
* "Natural gas prices had increased sharply from the end of February and remained elevated from April to June due to the conflict in the Middle East," the authority said.
* "An increase in the cost of natural gas will lead to an increase in the costs of producing electricity and town gas in Singapore," it added.
* It said the situation in the Middle East remains uncertain but if it improves, fuel prices could fall and lead to lower electricity and town gas tariffs in the fourth quarter. (US$1 = 1.2940 Singapore dollars)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia secures RM160mil export prospects at Korea expo
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia External Trade Development Corp (Matrade) has exceeded its export target at the Korea Import Expo (KIE) 2026, securing RM160 million in export prospects .
This exceeded Matrade's initial target of RM120 million, proving the strong market demand for Malaysian products in the competitive East Asia region.
Matrade chief executive officer Abu Bakar Yusof said the exceptional trade leads secured at KIE 2026 underscore the strong appeal of Malaysian products in the South Korean market.
This reaffirms the local exporters' ability to meet the high standards of Korean consumers.
"South Korea's private domestic consumption expenditure, forecasted at nearly US$924.1 billion in 2026, is a testament that the market offers promising opportunities for Malaysian products," Abu Bakar said in a statement.
According to Matrade, Malaysia has recorded the largest national participation among Asean countries at KIE 2026, ahead of regional peers such as Singapore, the Philippines, Myanmar, Cambodia, Vietnam, and Indonesia.
Capitalising on South Korea's appetite for premium and lifestyle alternatives, it said these companies captured buyers' attention with a diverse product range.
"Strong interest centred on premium halal-certified ready-to-eat and frozen foods, snacks, confectionery, sauces, condiments, detergents and household goods," it said.
To turn buyer interest into solid trade agreements, Matrade executed a targeted, high-impact promotional strategy across Seoul.
This involved coordinating intensive, face-to-face business matching sessions that paired Malaysian exporters directly with verified South Korean importers and distributors.
Matrade seamlessly integrated these physical meetings with localised digital marketing campaigns and live-streaming sessions featuring South Korean key opinion leaders who rapidly elevated brand visibility throughout the event.
The successful campaign stems from the memorandum of understanding signed between Matrade and the Korea Importers Association on Dec 1, 2024, during the official visit of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to South Korea.
By maintaining a consistent presence at KIE, Matrade continues to drive economic growth under Malaysia's Look East Policy.
With total bilateral trade expanding 6.8 per cent to reach RM117.12 billion (US$27.42 billion) in 2025, KIE opens a reliable pathway for Malaysian exporters aiming for long-term expansion in Northeast Asia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: 'Go ahead,' Onn Hafiz brushes off Puad
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has brushed off attacks levelled against him by former Rengit state assemblyman Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi, saying such tactics would not influence voters in the Johor election.
The Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman said he believed Puad's remarks would not sway public opinion.
"If you want to attack me… go ahead.
"But what matters most is that voters are not influenced by such tactics," he said while delivering his speech at the BN Batu Pahat leadership briefing in Rengit here today.
Also present were BN candidates Boo Chin Leong (Penggaram), Mohd Yusla Ismail (Senggarang) and Mohd Zaidi Japar (Rengit).
Onn Hafiz said Puad should instead follow the example of several Umno leaders who accepted the party's decision after being dropped as candidates.
He cited Datuk Ayub Jamil, who was replaced by Puad as the BN candidate for the Rengit seat in the 2022 Johor election, as an example.
"Datuk Ayub was not fielded previously, but he did not sulk.
"He never went around criticising others because this struggle is not about individuals; it is about the people and the community.
"We also learned from what was shared by the Umno secretary-general that the candidate he (Puad) wanted to put forward was his own son.
"So various attacks were made, including against me, and more seriously, he even linked it to the palace," he said.
Meanwhile, Onn Hafiz said Batu Pahat BN had no shortage of qualified candidates, making the selection process a challenging one.
"We can only choose three candidates.
"Each constituency can only have one candidate, which is why we selected those who are truly capable of winning the seat for the party," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New Malaysian passport introduces 94 security features, enhanced travel document protection [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The new Malaysian passport, set to be rolled out tomorrow, features 94 security elements, nearly double the previous version's 49, to strengthen protection against forgery, alteration and misuse.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the enhanced features include visual, covert and forensic security elements.
He said the new passport also introduces a 10-year validity option priced at RM350, while the five-year passport remains at RM200.
"Initially, the passport will be issued at 14 state offices, including the headquarters, during the first phase before being rolled out progressively until the seventh phase.
"With these enhanced security features, it is much safer and harder to forge," he told reporters here today.
Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim launched the new passport at the Immigration booth in the Dewan Rakyat lobby.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/06/1476681/malaysians-aged-18-and-above-can-choose-5-year-or-10-year-passport-july
Also present were Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail and the prime minister's wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.
Zakaria also presented the new passport to the prime minister during the launch.
He said the average monthly passport issuance in the country is between 180,000 and 220,000, and output is expected to increase due to high demand.
However, he advised Malaysians not to rush to replace valid passports.
"Malaysians do not need to rush to change their passports if the current ones are still valid. Only those whose passports have expired or those applying for new ones may proceed," he said.
According to the Home Ministry, the new passport structure marks a major step in strengthening travel document security while providing greater convenience and value for Malaysians.
Malaysians aged 18 and above can choose between a five-year or 10-year passport based on their needs from tomorrow.
The ministry said the 10-year passport would reduce renewal frequency, save time and offer savings of RM50 compared with renewing a five-year passport twice over the same period.
It added that the new passport incorporates security technologies in line with standards set by the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO), maintaining Malaysia's reputation for secure travel documents.
The new passport also features national identity elements inspired by Malaysia's history, cultural heritage and constitutional monarchy, including the Rajah Brooke's Birdwing butterfly as the new icon, replacing the hibiscus.
The ministry said new-generation printing machines, capable of producing up to 60 passports an hour, would support issuance needs.
It added that the upgraded passport reflects the government's commitment to ensuring Malaysia's travel document remains secure, modern, reliable and aligned with international best practices.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Senior citizen charged with sexually assaulting two schoolgirls in mosque storeroom
Article:
MELAKA: A senior citizen was charged at the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court today with two counts of committing physical sexual assault against two primary schoolgirls.
Aziz Mohammad @ Teh Eng Guan, 73, pleaded not guilty to the charges framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
The offence carries a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
The proceedings were heard before judge Raja Noor Adilla Raja Mahyaldin.
Deputy public prosecutor Rif'ah 'Izzati Abdul Mutalif appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was represented by S.S. Amritpal Singh.
On the first count, Aziz was alleged to have committed physical sexual assault against a 10-year-old girl by groping her private parts, hugging her, and kissing her cheek in the storeroom of Masjid Al-Abrar in Semabok here at about 1.05pm on June 15.
On the second count, the senior citizen was accused of committing the same act against an 8-year-old girl at the same location at about 1.05pm on June 22.
The prosecution offered bail at RM25,000 for each charge, with the additional condition that the accused must not disturb or approach the victims until the case is concluded.
However, defence counsel appealed for a lower amount, citing that the accused suffers from chronic diabetes, supports an autistic child, and poses no flight risk as his son-in-law would act as bailor.
The court allowed bail at RM10,000 for all charges with the conditions requested by the prosecution, and set July 29 for mention for the submission of documents.
Media reports previously revealed that the suspect allegedly lured the two girls with RM5 and instant noodles before assaulting them.
The acts were uncovered after the victims told their school counselling teacher.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar was reported as saying the suspect was believed to have committed the acts repeatedly in the mosque storeroom located near the victims' school over two weeks starting June 16.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM1.65m worth of counterfeit, contraband liquor seized in Klang Valley raids
Article:
Fuad Nizam
KUALA LUMPUR: Police seized RM1.65 million worth of counterfeit and non-duty-paid alcoholic drinks in two raids in Kepong Baru and Bandar Utama last Thursday.
The raids were carried out under Op Taring Alpha 2 by the federal police's Internal Security and Public Order Department's Wildlife Crime Bureau/ Special Investigation Intelligence (WCB/PSK), Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry Enforcement Division and the Sentul district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department Serious Crime Unit (D9).
WCB/PSK commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said the operation was launched based on coordinated intelligence and led to the arrest of a Malaysian man and a Sri Lankan man.
"The raids crippled a syndicate that used storage premises as locations for storing, selling and distributing branded counterfeit alcoholic drinks as well as those without Customs duty payments.
"This helped prevent losses in government tax revenue and directly cut off the syndicate's distribution network," he said in a statement.
Zaki said both suspects played important roles in the syndicate's logistics operations around the Klang Valley.
"Initial checks found that the first suspect, a Malaysian, admitted to acting as a coordinator and storekeeper since June 2025, receiving RM3,000 a month. He received direct instructions from a local syndicate leader known as Sathiya.
"The second suspect, a 30-year-old Sri Lankan, said he started working in January this year, earning RM2,500 a month as a storekeeper and delivery person supplying goods to buyers," he said.
He said police seized 11,496 cans and 9,737 bottles of various well-known alcoholic drink brands in the market, suspected to be counterfeit.
"The raiding party also seized four vehicles, comprising three bonded lorries and a van used for distribution purposes, bringing the total value of the seizure and confiscated items to RM1,656,140.
"All suspects and seized items have since been handed over to the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry's Putrajaya Enforcement Division for further investigation," he said.
He said the case was being investigated under Section 102 of the Trademarks Act 2019 for the offence of using registered trademarks incorrectly or falsely, which carries a minimum fine of RM1,000 for each item upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MOF: Health Ministry spending adjustment capped at RM500m, not RM3.1b as claimed online
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Ministry of Finance (MOF) has considered an adjustment to the Health Ministry’s operating expenditure of up to RM500 million, far below the RM3.1 billion figure circulating on social media, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan.
He said the amount does not involve core spending, including emoluments, medical supplies, overtime payments and on-call allowances.
“Medicines allocation has also been increased to RM6.5 billion for 2026.
“For 2026, the Health Ministry continues to target the recruitment of more than 18,000 healthcare workers, with no reduction from the previous year,” he said in the Dewan Rakyat during Question Time.
